response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced senior engineer with strong testing culture, open-source leadership, and some high-throughput systems exposure (DLang microservices handling millions of requests/second at Sociomantic). However, the role's core minimum qualification — 8 years of C++ — is far from met: documented C++ work amounts to roughly 7 months at National Instruments plus a thesis project. His infrastructure experience is application-level rather than building large-scale cloud compute platforms, and he has no formal technical-leadership role mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers. The profile is a mismatch for a C++-centric cloud compute platform lead.
strengths: Broad, hands-on engineering experience across web, embedded, and low-latency backend systems
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, open-source test tooling, and community mentoring via workshops
Experience with high-throughput distributed microservices and Kubernetes-deployable platforms
concerns: Falls drastically short of the 8-year C++ requirement (~1 year of professional C/C++)
No production cloud compute platform or large-scale infrastructure ownership experience
No formal technical-lead role managing priorities, deadlines, and cross-team alignment
